artifacts.role: ADR appendix defining public shell retirement states and durable-claim requirements
ownership model documentation for bridge_shell through whole_file_owned shell states
compiler operations documentation linking WPHX PHP durable claims to shell-retirement evidence
root agent directive requiring durable public PHP claims to cite the shell-retirement ladder
expanded agent guidance for copied, transformed, hand-authored, and JS-patched public PHP shells
program rollup recording the shell-retirement policy under WPHX-COMP

claims: Copied, transformed, hand-authored, and JS-patched public PHP shells are bridge mechanisms, not durable public PHP ownership evidence.

The repository now defines allowed claims and removal gates for bridge_shell, generated_helper_with_temporary_shell, compiler_emitted_original_path_shell, durable_public_adapter, and whole_file_owned states.
Future durable public PHP claims must cite compiler-emitted original-path shell evidence, durable adapter evidence, whole-file evidence, or an accepted backend/custom-target improvement.
If a temporary shell would be reused for a second durable claim class, the policy requires a blocker and WPHX PHP or backend-lane promotion rather than normalizing the bridge.
non_claims: This receipt does not promote any existing copied or JS-patched shell to durable public PHP ownership.
This receipt does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership, mixed PHP/HTML template ownership, or full WP_Http::request ownership.
This receipt does not implement new compiler emission features.
